---
title: Young firms drive job creation across the nation
description: Density plot showing the distribution of job creation by young firms
  (0-5 years) across rural and nonrural counties.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-job-creation-density/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Entrepreneurship
  - Labor Market
tags:
  - BDS
  - Job Creation
  - Young Firms
  - Entrepreneurship
  - Rural vs. Nonrural
  - Density Plot
image: job_creation_density.svg
chart:
  title: Young firms drive job creation across the nation
  chartType: density
  interactive: false
  dataSource: U.S. Census Bureau, Business Dynamics Statistics
  sourceUrl: https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/bds.html
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - census-bds
usesPackages:
  - cori-data-bds
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This density plot shows the county-level distribution of job creation attributable to young firms (0-5 years old), comparing rural and nonrural areas.](./job_creation_density.svg)

## Overview

This density plot shows the county-level distribution of job creation attributable to young firms (0-5 years old), comparing rural and nonrural areas.

## Key Findings

- Young firms account for a substantial share of job creation in both rural and nonrural areas
- The distribution of young-firm job creation is similar across rurality
- Median share of job creation from young firms is approximately 40%

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/job_creation_density.R` in the producing project.
